steps.title: Evaluate each choice against the table
steps.content:
• Choice A — 123.: Bear 123 is 7 years old — not the youngest bear in the table. ✗
  • Choice B — 157.: Bear 157 is 6 years old — older than bear 122. ✗
  • Choice C — 117.: Bear 117 is 11 years old — the oldest bear in the table. ✗
  • Choice D — 122.: Bear 122 is 3 years old — the smallest value in the Age column, making it the youngest. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the minimum age
steps.content: The four ages in the table are 3, 6, 7, and 11 years. The minimum is 3, which belongs to bear 122. That confirms D is correct.
